# Data Protection Impact Assessment

## Scope and processing

This service is likely to be accessed by children, so a DPIA is required
under Standard 2. This section describes the personal data we collect, the
purposes of the processing, and the data flows between the app, our backend,
and any third parties. We record account identifiers, progress data, and
limited device information. No special category data is collected.

## Necessity and proportionality

We have assessed the necessity and proportionality of every processing
activity. Data is collected only where it is needed to deliver the feature a
child has chosen to use, retained for the minimum period, and never used for
advertising or behavioural profiling. Where a less intrusive option exists we
take it instead.

## Risks to children

We identified the main risks to children: unexpected contact, exposure to
commercial pressure, and the collection of more data than a child would
reasonably expect. Each risk is rated by likelihood and severity.

## Mitigations

For every risk we set out a mitigation: private-by-default settings, no
outbound links into the parent browser session, on-device handling of sensor
input, and clear age-appropriate explanations of what happens to a child's
data. Residual risk is reviewed before each release.
